summary: The new file adds useful happy-path coverage, but two mission-critical scenarios are effectively untested: it stubs out the real scoop/tool path behind `createContext`, and the CLI-vs-extension parity case reuses the same bootstrap logic for both labels.

issues.description: The integration file never exercises the real `ScoopContext` path, so it does not actually cover the promised command → hook → bridge → scoop flow or validate real cone-tool bypass behavior.
issues.evidence: `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-integration.test.ts:145-206` defines `makeMockProvider()` as a fake `AgentBridgeContext` with no-op `init()` and synthetic `prompt()` / `getAgentMessages()`, and every scenario injects that seam via `harness.publish({ createContext, ... })` (`:317-322`, `:357-362`, `:387-393`, `:466-471`, `:576-581`, `:626-631`, `:660-665`). The real bridge path would construct `new ScoopContext(...)` in `packages/webapp/src/scoops/agent-bridge.ts:120-129`, where the actual management tools are assembled inside `packages/webapp/src/scoops/scoop-context.ts:172-181`. Scenario 5 then wraps a detached `createScoopManagementTools()` array in `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-integration.test.ts:496-518`; those tool instances are never passed into the bridge or the mocked context, so the zero counters do not prove the real cone tool surface was bypassed.
issues.suggestedFix: Keep this file on the bridge's default `ScoopContext` path and mock only the model/provider layer. If a seam is still needed, add at least one scenario that uses the real `ScoopContext` and attach the counter instrumentation to the actual tool-construction/execution path.

issues.description: The CLI-vs-extension parity scenario is vacuous because both "harnesses" execute the same bootstrap logic; the label does not change behavior, so the test cannot catch divergence between the CLI and extension entry points.
issues.evidence: `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-integration.test.ts:257-276` shows `startBootstrap(label)` always doing the same `makeOrchestrator()` + `orch.init()` + `publishAgentBridge(...)` setup, and `label` is only stored on the returned object. The parity test then calls that same helper twice in `:565-616`. It never imports or exercises the distinct runtime call sites in `packages/webapp/src/ui/main.ts:1158-1163` and `packages/chrome-extension/src/offscreen.ts:71-78`, so a regression in either bootstrap path would still leave this test green.
issues.suggestedFix: Build genuinely separate CLI and extension harnesses that go through the runtime-specific bridge-publication wiring (or spy on those entry-point call sites) before asserting identical agent behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `webapp-worker` skill should explicitly distinguish "mock provider" from "mock `createContext`" for full-stack `agent` tests.
sharedStateObservations.why: This feature replaced the real `ScoopContext` with a fake `AgentBridgeContext` yet still read as "full stack" to the worker. A short note would prevent future workers from accidentally cutting the scoop/tool path out of integration tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `webapp-worker` parity guidance should require distinct CLI and extension harnesses (or explicit entry-point spies) when a feature claims runtime parity.
sharedStateObservations.why: The current guidance allowed a label-only harness to satisfy the parity requirement even though it never exercised `ui/main.ts` versus `offscreen.ts`.
